body.para: A cold boot is a fixed sequence of stages from reset to the interactive prompt. Each instruction along the way is carried through the six in-order pipeline stages (IF, DE, GR, EX, MEM, WB) and commits at Writeback (see Overview); the stages below are the firmware-level milestones.

list.li: Reset / SROM hand-off. The machine resets with the SROM parameters in registers R16-R21 and enters the firmware startup.
PAL and CPU setup. The startup establishes PAL_BASE, sets the I_CTL SDE bits, flushes the ITB / DTB and instruction cache, and primes the shadow registers.
Decompression. The firmware inflates its own compressed image (about 4M cycles; see Firmware Decode & Decompression).
Relocation. A memory-copy moves the decompressed firmware to its run location and jumps to the SRM console entry.

SRM power-up. Banner and memory sizing; the PCI / ISA probe; GCT / FRU initialization; the option-firmware scan of the CD and floppy; the Loadable Firmware Update utility (UPD>); system and disk testing; and finally the interactive >>> prompt. (That visible sequence is detailed in Reaching the SRM Prompt.)

body.para: The cold boot reaches the >>> prompt and is fully interactive. The remaining runtime work downstream of decompression centers on a clock-interrupt return path (the hw_ret R2 sequence) and, for booting a guest, the front-panel halt gate and PCI device enumeration. Those are tracked in Current Development Status.
